albums.title: Discovery
albums.reason: With Digital Love, One More Time, and Superheroes among your most-played tracks ever, Discovery is the album you built those obsessions on — it deserves a full front-to-back revisit.

albums.title: In Rainbows
albums.reason: Reckoner is your third most-played track of all time, and it lives here — In Rainbows is Radiohead at their most emotionally direct and it may have been a while since you sat with the whole thing.

albums.title: Wish You Were Here
albums.reason: Pink Floyd sits at nearly 1,200 all-time plays for you, and this record is one of their most emotionally concentrated — a perfect vault pull for a slow evening.

albums.title: Melody A.M.
albums.reason: Röyksopp has over 1,000 plays in your all-time chart and Melody A.M. is their most quietly beautiful record — lush, slow-burning, and worth returning to.

albums.title: Moon Safari
albums.reason: Air is a bedrock artist for you with over 1,000 plays, and Moon Safari remains their most iconic and cohesive statement — comfort listening at its finest.

albums.title: Leftism
albums.reason: Release the Pressure is your fourth most-played track ever and it opens Leftism — with over 1,000 all-time plays, this album is clearly part of your DNA and deserves a full session.

albums.title: In Sides
albums.reason: Orbital sits at 777 all-time plays and In Sides is widely considered their magnum opus — immersive, sprawling, and the kind of record that rewards a proper listen.

albums.title: Overgrown
albums.reason: James Blake is in your all-time top 20 and Postpone and CMYK are among your most-played tracks — Overgrown is his richest album and probably worth pulling back out of the vault.

albums.title: Galactic Melt
albums.reason: Your love of Daft Punk and Justice points directly at Com Truise — this debut is packed with slow-motion electro that feels like a lost transmission from a retrofuturist world you'd inhabit.

albums.title: Black Sands
albums.reason: You liked Bonobo's Kiara, and Black Sands is where that sound crystallised — jazzy, beat-driven and deeply melodic in a way that sits perfectly alongside your Air and Röyksopp love.

albums.title: Immunity
albums.reason: Your deep runs with Radiohead and Underworld suggest you love electronic music with genuine emotional weight — Immunity is a landmark record that bridges those worlds with devastating effect.

albums.title: Dummy
albums.reason: With Massive Attack in your recent listening and the dark cinematic thread running through Radiohead and James Blake, Dummy is an unmissable classic that belongs in your collection.

albums.title: Untrue
albums.reason: Your love of Underworld, James Blake, and the more spectral side of electronic music points straight at Burial's Untrue — it's one of the most emotionally singular records of the 2000s.

albums.title: Swim
albums.reason: If you love the lysergic quality of Radiohead's later work and the groove of Daft Punk, Caribou's Swim is a record that lives at that exact intersection — lush, hypnotic and deeply satisfying.

albums.title: Mezzanine
albums.reason: Massive Attack appears in your recent 12-month listening and your all-time taste runs through heavy atmospheric electronic music — Mezzanine is their darkest and most cinematic record and likely new territory for you to dig into properly.

albums.title: Feed Me Weird Things
albums.reason: Your all-time love of Orbital, Leftfield, and complex electronic music suggests you'd respond to Squarepusher's debut — it fuses jazz bass virtuosity with breakbeats in a way that's still unlike anything else.

albums.title: Float
albums.reason: Your recent deep listening to Nils Frahm, Hania Rani, and Ben Lukas Boysen shows a clear love of intimate solo piano — Peter Broderick's Float sits in exactly that space, tender and understated.

albums.title: Knee-Deep in the North Sea
albums.reason: Your Oscar Peterson Trio plays and recent jazz exploration, alongside the ambient-leaning electronic music you've been in, suggest Portico Quartet would resonate — they blend jazz with dreamy minimalism beautifully.

albums.title: Elaenia
albums.reason: With Four Tet, Weval, and Max Cooper in your recent rotation, and your growing jazz interest via Oscar Peterson and Bill Evans, Elaenia sits at the perfect crossroads — organic, spacious and deeply musical.

albums.title: Satin Doll
albums.reason: Your recent listening arc — Oscar Peterson, Bill Evans, Nils Frahm, and ambient electronic — points toward Sam Gendel's quietly radical take on jazz standards, which sounds like nothing else and would likely stop you in your tracks.

albums.title: Choose Your Weapon
albums.reason: Your recent listening to Sault, Charles Bradley, and Bill Withers alongside electronic music suggests you'd love Hiatus Kaiyote's polyrhythmic neo-soul — this album is an all-time great in the genre.

albums.title: Black Focus
albums.reason: The combination of Oscar Peterson, Four Tet, and Sault in your recent listening creates a clear appetite for jazz with groove and electronic texture — Black Focus delivers exactly that with tremendous energy.

albums.title: At the Pershing: But Not for Me
albums.reason: Your Oscar Peterson Trio plays and Bill Evans listening show a developing love of classic jazz piano — Ahmad Jamal's live masterpiece is essential in that lineage and would fit perfectly alongside Night Train.

albums.title: Alpe Lusia
albums.reason: Your recent listening to Dominik Eulberg, Super Flu, and Max Cooper shows a love of techno with natural textures and emotional depth — Stimming's Alpe Lusia lives in that same beautiful space.

albums.title: The Chocolate Elvis
albums.reason: Tosca is your single most-played artist this week with 30 plays and Suzuki has been in heavy rotation — The Chocolate Elvis is their earliest classic and a perfect companion piece you haven't recently revisited.

albums.title: Mirage
albums.reason: Ben Lukas Boysen is your second most active artist this week at 23 plays and you've been deep in Gravity and Spells — Mirage is his most recent full statement and the logical next step in that listening thread.

albums.title: The Universe Smiles Upon You
albums.reason: Khruangbin has been a strong signal this week and Con Todo El Mundo is already in your recent play history — their debut is equally hypnotic and a natural place to dig deeper right now.

albums.title: In the Rain
albums.reason: Neal Francis hit 12 plays this week and Return to Zero has been in your recent rotation — In the Rain is his most fully realised record and would slot perfectly into your current soulful, warm mood.

albums.title: Tripping with Nils Frahm
albums.reason: You've been playing Tripping with Nils Frahm tracks almost exclusively in your most recent session — this live document deserves to be heard as the full album experience it was designed to be.

albums.artist: A Winged Victory for the Sullen
albums.title: Atomos
albums.reason: A Winged Victory for the Sullen has been active this week and The Undivided Five is already in your recent play history — Atomos is their most immersive and cinematic work and would suit your current meditative headspace perfectly.

albums.title: Prehension
albums.reason: Joep Beving has been one of your top artists this month at 15 plays and Liminal has been a go-to album — Prehension is his debut and the starting point for that whole sound, worth sitting with right now.

albums.title: Starfruit
albums.reason: Moonchild appeared in your recent listening and Voyager has been in play — Starfruit is their most polished and warmly produced record, and it fits your current soft, soulful evening energy beautifully.

songs.title: Harder, Better, Faster, Stronger
songs.reason: You've played Discovery-era Daft Punk relentlessly — Digital Love, One More Time, Superheroes — and this track is the mechanical counterweight to all of them, a vault classic worth revisiting.

songs.title: All I Need
songs.reason: Reckoner is your third most-played track ever and lives on In Rainbows alongside this — All I Need is one of the most emotionally devastating things Radiohead ever recorded and deserves its own spotlight.

songs.title: Cowgirl
songs.reason: Born Slippy is in your all-time top 20 tracks and Underworld has 633 plays — Cowgirl is the other side of their peak, an eight-minute ecstatic build that hits just as hard.

songs.title: Remind Me
songs.reason: Röyksopp has over 1,000 all-time plays and Melody A.M. is the album this comes from — Remind Me is one of the great electronic pop songs of its era and a perfect vault pull.

songs.title: Shine On You Crazy Diamond (Parts I–V)
songs.reason: Pink Floyd is a bedrock artist for you with nearly 1,200 plays and this track from Wish You Were Here is one of rock's most emotionally transcendent pieces — comfort food from the deepest part of the vault.

songs.title: Phantom
songs.reason: Justice has 771 all-time plays and their debut is one of your foundational electronic records — Phantom is the album's most cinematic and brooding moment, a track that hits differently at volume.

songs.title: The End Has No End
songs.reason: The Strokes sit at 652 all-time plays and Room on Fire deserves a revisit — The End Has No End is one of their most propulsive and underrated tracks from that era.

songs.title: The Rain Song
songs.reason: Led Zeppelin has nearly 1,000 all-time plays for you and this is one of their most emotionally generous and overlooked pieces — a vault gem that rewards a quiet listen.

songs.title: Open Eye Signal
songs.reason: Your love of Orbital, Leftfield, and the more euphoric end of Daft Punk points straight at this track — a 10-minute build that stands among the finest electronic pieces of the last 20 years.

songs.title: Archangel
songs.reason: The spectral emotional weight in your James Blake and Underworld listening suggests Burial would resonate deeply — Archangel is his most accessible and beautiful track and a perfect entry point.

songs.title: Sun
songs.reason: Your Radiohead and Daft Punk combination points at artists who blend the cerebral with the euphoric — Sun from Swim is exactly that, a hypnotic loop that keeps unfolding the longer you stay inside it.

songs.title: Glory Box
songs.reason: With the cinematic, heavy emotional thread running through your all-time favourites, Glory Box is one of the most iconic songs in that space — if you haven't heard it properly, this is the moment.

songs.title: Ether Drift
songs.reason: Your all-time love of Daft Punk and the slower, more introspective electronic tracks like Veridis Quo suggests Com Truise's slower material would hit hard — this is one of his most gorgeous pieces.

songs.title: My Red Hot Car
songs.reason: The quirky, complex edge of your electronic taste — Orbital, Justice, the stranger corners of Daft Punk — suggests you'd love Squarepusher's most approachable and joyful track, which sounds like nothing else.

songs.title: Kiara
songs.reason: You explicitly liked Kiara as a signal track — this is the confirmation pick, highlighting it as the standout discovery gem it is from Black Sands and the anchor of that whole recommendation.

songs.title: Locked
songs.reason: Four Tet appears in your recent 12-month listening but not your all-time top 20, suggesting you're still exploring — Locked from There Is Love in You is one of his finest and most emotionally rich pieces.

songs.title: LesAlpx
songs.reason: Your recent Four Tet and Weval listening alongside growing jazz interest via Oscar Peterson makes this track from Elaenia a perfect bridge — graceful, melodic and quietly complex.

songs.title: Nakamarra
songs.reason: Your recent listening to Sault, Charles Bradley, and Bill Withers shows a love of deeply soulful music with texture — Nakamarra is one of the most beautiful neo-soul songs of its generation.

songs.title: It's All Becoming Clear to Me
songs.reason: Your deep recent runs with Nils Frahm, Hania Rani, and Felix Rösch show a love of intimate, emotionally unguarded piano music — this track captures that mood perfectly.

songs.title: But Not for Me
songs.reason: Your Oscar Peterson Trio and Bill Evans listening is deepening a clear love of classic jazz piano — this live performance is one of the most joyful and swinging in the entire canon.

songs.title: Strings of Light
songs.reason: The Four Tet and Oscar Peterson combination in your recent listening suggests you'd love this track's blend of jazz harmony and electronic rhythm — it's a head-nodder from start to finish.

songs.title: Sog
songs.reason: Your recent Dominik Eulberg and Super Flu plays show a love of techno that breathes and has natural texture — Sog from Alpe Lusia is one of the most quietly stunning tracks in that space.

songs.title: I Remember You
songs.reason: Your Bill Evans and Nils Frahm combination shows a love of introspective, slightly unconventional music — Sam Gendel's treatment of this jazz standard is haunting and unlike anything you've likely heard.

songs.title: Ruins
songs.reason: Your recent balance of jazz, ambient piano, and textured electronic music creates a perfect opening for this track — Ruins is hypnotic and emotionally open in a way that fits your current listening arc.

songs.title: Honey
songs.reason: Tosca is your most-played artist this week by a clear margin and The Chocolate Elvis is the album — Honey is the standout track on it, warm and unhurried in exactly the mood you're in.

songs.title: Maria También
songs.reason: Khruangbin has been a strong signal this week and you've been in Con Todo El Mundo — Maria También is the album's most seductive and hypnotic moment and a perfect standalone listen.

songs.title: Alameda
songs.reason: Neal Francis has been playing heavily this week and In the Rain is the album recommendation — Alameda is its standout track, a slow-burning soul anthem that fits your warm current headspace perfectly.

songs.title: All Melody
songs.reason: Your most recent tracks are almost all Nils Frahm and you've been deep in Tripping and Spaces — All Melody from the album of the same name is a different, more orchestral side of him that would broaden the current session.

songs.artist: A Winged Victory for the Sullen
songs.title: Steep Hills of Vicodin Tears
songs.reason: A Winged Victory for the Sullen appeared in your last 7 days and Atomos is the album pick — this track from it is one of their most emotionally expansive and sits perfectly in a late-evening mood.

songs.title: Sleeping Lotus
songs.reason: Joep Beving has been one of your most active artists this month and Prehension is the album pick — Sleeping Lotus is its most quietly devastating track and will feel completely at home in your current mood.

songs.title: Nocturne
songs.reason: Ben Lukas Boysen is your second most-played artist this week and you've been through both Gravity and Spells — Nocturne from Mirage is a perfect next step, sitting between his piano and electronic worlds.

songs.title: The List
songs.reason: Moonchild appeared in your recent listening and Starfruit is the album pick — The List is the record's most tender and fully-formed moment, warm and intimate in exactly the way your current listening mood calls for.

djSets.setName: HMWL Podcast 430: Niconé & David Hasert (Kompakt / Get Physical / HMWL)
Daphni - FOLD, London. Nov 22, 2025. 7hr DJ set
Klausmarie b2b Alec Troniq - RAVIN' RAVIN' 2026 @ Sektor Evolution
